We ordered food for 10 people at 12 PM during Ramadan so it would arrive by iftar.

We called the restaurant at 5 PM before iftar, and they told us the order had already left and was on the way.

We kept calling and trying to reach them, but no one answered from 5:50 PM until 8 PM.

The food never arrived, and no one contacted us.

We also sent them a message on Facebook Messenger. They replied saying the delivery would arrive in a few minutes and that the driver would call us; but no one called and the food never came. Extremely disrespectful service.

They finally called at 9:30 PM, surprised that we were upset, and they didn’t even know that the food had never arrived.

One of the worst experiences ever, and the last time we order from them.

بصراحة كانت تجربة استثنائية بفضل الممرضة إسراء. من أول لحظة وهي بتتعامل بأقصى درجات الرحمة والاهتمام والاحترافية بشكل يميزها فعلًا. كانت دايمًا موجودة، منتبهة لكل تفصيلة، وبتسبق احتياجاتي قبل ما أطلبها.

أنا اتعاملت مع أنظمة رعاية صحية في أمريكا وإنجلترا، وأقدر أقول بثقة إن مستوى الرعاية اللي بتقدمه الممرضة إسراء مش بس على نفس المستوى، ده كمان أعلى وأفضل. مهارتها الطبية، وهدوءها، وطيبتها الحقيقية بيخلّوا أي مريض يحس بالراحة والأمان.

هي فعلًا مثال للتمريض على مستوى عالمي، والمستشفى محظوظة جدًا بوجودها.

نظراً لجفاف بشرتي الشديد مؤخراً، حجزتُ جلسة ترطيب عميق. مع ذلك، اقتصرت معظم الجلسة على تنظيف عميق مع ترطيب طفيف. خضعتُ لجلسة تقشير دقيق، وتنظيف بالملعقة، وإزالة الرؤوس السوداء، وجلسة تفتيح البشرة. لكن من الواضح أن المعالجة لم تكن تجيد استخدام جهاز التقشير الدقيق والملعقة بشكل صحيح؛ كانت العملية مؤلمة للغاية، وشعرتُ بألم شديد في بشرتي. هذا النوع من العلاج يُلحق الضرر بالطبقة القرنية من الجلد. طلبتُ أيضاً جلسة مانيكير في نفس الوقت، بتكلفة 300 جنيه مصري. لكنها اقتصرت على تقليم الجلد المحيط بالأظافر فقط، دون وضع طلاء أظافر. بعد طلبي، وضعت طلاء الأظافر بشكل سطحي، ما لم يكن مانيكيراً على الإطلاق. علاوة على ذلك، استغرق طلاء الأظافر وقتاً طويلاً ليجف (10 دقائق) وكان غير متناسق. إضافة إلى ذلك، كان روتين العناية بالبشرة لديها دون المستوى المطلوب، ومع ذلك طلبت 2200 جنيه مصري. أعتقد أنها لم تتخرج من تدريبها الطبي. إنها لا تعرف كيفية استخدام أدوات التجميل بشكل صحيح لتقديم تجربة خدمة أفضل لزبائنها.

review_text: The food was very good overall. However, I gave one less star because I specifically requested all six burgers to be beef, but one was chicken instead. Additionally, the burger that didn’t have cheese or sauce was not clearly marked on the wrapper.

I used to visit this salon from time to time and previously had positive experiences because the stylists were attentive to my needs and vision. Unfortunately, my most recent visit was very disappointing. The hairstylist/owner, Joseph, did not seem to actively listen to what I was asking for and appeared to be rushing through the appointment.

Despite clearly stating that maintaining length was my top priority, my hair was cut much shorter than requested and the bangs turned out poorly. I also mentioned several times that cutting my hair while wet might not be the best approach since my hair is curly, but after some insistence I trusted his recommendation. Unfortunately, the final result did not turn out well.

I also found some of the comments about my hair being dry unnecessary and somewhat embarrassing, especially when they were made “jokingly” in front of other staff members, despite me already explaining the reason.

I also noticed that videos were being taken during the appointment without my consent, which made me uncomfortable.

Considering the outcome and the experience overall, the service felt overpriced and disappointing. Based on this visit, I would not recommend this salon, particularly for people with curly hair. I hope the salon takes this feedback seriously and becomes more attentive to clients’ requests in the future.

My 11-day stay at Nile X was extremely stressful and disappointing. Before booking my flight, I had communicated through the Booking.com chat that I would arrive around 5 am and asked if early check-in would be possible. I received an “ok” response, so I planned my travel accordingly. However, when I arrived I was told early check-in was not possible and was asked to pay an additional 60 USD to access the room between 6 am and noon. When I showed screenshots of the conversation, the owner denied that early check-in had been confirmed.
The location was also misleading. I had been told it would be very close to Zamalek X Hostel, but it turned out to be on a quiet and uncomfortable street, and the property itself is on the 19th floor, something that was never mentioned. The elevator felt unsafe and unstable.
The “Nile view” room description was also inaccurate. The windows were not double-glazed, so the room was extremely noisy, and the actual Nile view was minimal.
Several basic amenities advertised were missing or poor:
Breakfast advertised as included, but in practice there was almost nothing provided.
Kitchen was poorly equipped and unhygienic, lacking basic items like a proper frying pan, knife, or mugs. I ended up buying my own ingredients and making my own breakfast most days.
Bathroom lacked essentials, including toilet paper.
The bed was unstable, with loose wooden planks supporting the mattress. This was only partially fixed near the end of my stay.
Staff frequently asked me to pay the extra fee in cash and knocked repeatedly on my door, which felt uncomfortable and intrusive.
Overall, the stay involved misleading information, poor maintenance, lack of hygiene, and stressful interactions with management. Unfortunately, this experience made my time in Cairo far more difficult than it should have been.
